xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: NBCC Bhawan, Lodhi Road, New Delhi- 110003
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: NBCC Bhawan, Lodhi Road, New Delhi- 110003
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: The Company provides management and consultancy for a range of civil construction projects including residential and commercial complexes, re-development of old government colonies, education and medical institutions, infrastructure project roads, water supply systems, storm water systems and water storage solutions.

NBCC operates in the Infrastructure Segment as well, wherein it executes projects such as High-Rise Chimneys, Cooling Towers, Coal Handling Plants etc. Under EPC, our services include Project conceptualization, Feasibility studies, Detailed Project Reports, Tender Specifications and various packages, Basic and Detailed Engineering, Review of Projects, Procurement, Construction, Drawings, commissioning & Testing, and handing it over to clients in ready to use and functional conditions.

Real Estate Development focuses primarily on residential and commercial projects such as corporate office buildings and commercial complexes. Real Estate activities includes Development of Real Estate Projects like Residential, Commercial and Institutional projects.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: NBCC caters to a wide range of clients including Central and State Government Ministries, Public Sector Undertakings, Municipal Bodies, and Financial Institutions. It also undertakes overseas projects funded by the Ministry of External Affairs and EXIM Bank in countries like Maldives, Mauritius, Seychelles, and Saudi Arabia. In addition, NBCC serves corporate clients and institutions in sectors such as Real Estate, Education, Healthcare, and Infrastructure. Its services span project management consultancy, engineering procurement and construction, and real estate development. The Company’s integrated capabilities enable it to deliver end-to-end infrastructure solutions across diverse customer segments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Real Estate Development and Construction Corporation of Rajasthan Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: Complaints as received on the CPGRAMS Portal. NBCC utilizes the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System, an Indian Government initiative to address the grievances of the public. Further, the expression communities include even those stakeholders which are not specifically identifiable in rest of the enlisted stakeholders such as Civil Actors, Media, anonymous complaints etc. The total number of 369 outstanding complaints were there during the financial year includes 33 nos. of pending complaints of previous year as on 31.03.2024. Further, total 325 complaints have been resolved during the FY 2024-25.

Complaints as received on the CPGRAMS Portal. NBCC utilizes the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System, an Indian Government initiative to address the grievances of the public. Further, the expression communities include even those stakeholders which are not specifically identifiable in rest of the enlisted stakeholders such as Civil Actors, Media, anonymous complaints etc. The total number of 262 complaints includes the pending complaints of previous year as on 31.03.2023. Further, total 229 complaints have been resolved during the FY 2023-24.
The pending complaint has also been resolved.

NBCC is having HR Manual and the same has uploaded on the website of the Company which includes procedures and Grievance Redressal Mechanism for Employees & Workers. Further, the Company has Policy for internal & external stakeholders grievance redressal which is also uploaded on the website of the Company. 2 Complaints were filed in FY 2024-25 and both the complaints have been resolved.

NBCC is having HR Manual and the same has uploaded on the website of the Company which includes procedures and Grievance Redressal Mechanism for Employees & Workers. Further, the Company has Policy for internal & external stakeholders grievance redressal which is also uploaded on the website of the Company.
The 15 complaints were received during FY 2024-25 w.r.t. Real Estate through CPGRAM/Public Grievance Portal.
8 complaints addressed and resolved during FY 2024–25.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk: Climate change, mainly caused by human activities, continues to impact the environment and the construction sector. As a company involved in project management and construction, NBCC’s operations contribute to greenhouse gas emissions, which add to these challenges. Climate change can affect the performance and durability of buildings, leading to higher maintenance needs and risks for occupants. It also brings changing weather patterns, which create challenges in energy use, building design, and planning.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NBCC implements energy management practices, including regular energy audits and the use of renewable energy, to reduce the risk of exceeding projected greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.Tree plantation initiatives are undertaken in line with relevant statutory requirements to support environmental sustainability and contribute to ecological balance.Air pollution monitoring is carried out at all project sites to ensure compliance with legal standards and to improve ambient air quality. NBCC also monitors and reports GHG emissions regularly to evaluate progress and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Opportunity: Waste management presents a valuable opportunity for NBCC, offering environmental benefits such as reduced pollution, resource conservation, and potential for recycling and waste-to-energy initiatives. It also brings economic gains through cost savings, regulatory compliance, and job creation. Strengthening waste management practices supports public health and encourages innovation in climate action. As NBCC is in the early stages of assessing its waste inputs and outputs, there is a clear opportunity to enhance existing systems and implement more sustainable waste solutions. Risk: At the same time, improper waste management poses environmental and health risks, including pollution, contamination of natural resources, and contribution to climate change. Addressing these risks is essential to safeguard ecosystems, ensure regulatory compliance, and protect community well-being.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NBCC adopts solid waste management (SWM) practices to effectively handle waste generated across its operations. Emphasis is placed on waste segregation at the source, supported by regular monitoring and accurate record-keeping to ensure compliance, traceability, and improved waste handling efficiency.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Opportunity: Embracing water management practices allows construction companies like NBCC to differentiate themselves in the market, attract environmentally conscious clients, and demonstrate a commitment to innovation and sustainability. It also strengthens business resilience by improving water efficiency across project sites and supports adaptation to the challenges posed by climate change, especially in water-stressed regions where NBCC operates. Risk: Construction activities such as site development, excavation, and material use can significantly impact local water resources and ecosystems, highlighting the need for effective environmental and water management strategies. Without proper controls, these impacts can lead to resource depletion, pollution, and non-compliance with environmental regulations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NBCC recognises the importance of using water wisely, especially in construction activities where demand can be high. The Company is working towards adopting more sustainable practices and water-saving technologies at its project sites. It also keeps track of water consumption and withdrawals, and wherever possible, looks for practical ways to reduce usage, as well as to reuse and recycle water. These efforts help lower the impact on local water resources and support long-term environmental sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk: At NBCC, ensuring the health and safety of the workforce is a core priority. While comprehensive safety guidelines are already in place to maintain a safe working environment, there is a need to enhance and update existing systems and practices to address evolving risks more effectively. Strengthening these areas is important to prevent potential health and safety incidents and to maintain a safe, reliable workplace for everyone involved in NBCC’s operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: NBCC has initiated the implementation of ISO 45001: Occupational Health and Safety Management System, which will help strengthen the safety framework across its operations and keep safety-related risks under control. In addition, efforts are being made to enhance existing systems by establishing a formal grievance mechanism, supported by dedicated grievance policies and standard operating procedures, to ensure that health and safety concerns are addressed in a structured and timely manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Opportunity: NBCC provides employee benefits in line with applicable government norms. However, there is an opportunity to further enhance employee support through additional training and awareness programs. By focusing on key topics aligned with the National Guidelines on Responsible Business Conduct (NGRBC) principles, NBCC aims to empower its workforce and strengthen its commitment to responsible and ethical business practices.

Opportunity: NBCC has the potential to strengthen its corporate social responsibility by actively engaging in initiatives that benefit local communities. Such involvement can lead to meaningful stakeholder engagement, including building relationships with local authorities, nonprofit organizations, and community members. These connections can support regulatory compliance, enhance reputation, help attract and retain talent, open doors to new markets and clients, and encourage collaboration and innovation in project execution.

Opportunity: Prioritizing ethical behavior enhances NBCC’s credibility, reputation, and competitive advantage. It helps reduce risks, attract top talent, and build lasting customer trust. Ethical conduct also strengthens stakeholder relationships, encouraging collaboration and the creation of shared value. By maintaining integrity in its business practices, NBCC can establish a strong foundation for long-term growth and position itself as a leader in the industry. To further reinforce its governance framework, NBCC is currently in the process of implementing a robust Anti-Bribery Management System.

Opportunity: Strong corporate governance enables effective decision-making, sound risk management, enhanced stakeholder confidence, improved access to capital, regulatory compliance, and long-term sustainability. By strengthening its governance framework, NBCC can improve overall performance, build trust among stakeholders, and position itself for long-term success in a dynamic business environment.

Risk: Implementing a sustainable supply chain in the construction industry involves several risks that NBCC must address. These include supplier reliability, cost escalations, supply chain disruptions, compliance with regulatory requirements, potential reputational risks, and challenges in collaborative coordination. Effectively managing these risks is crucial to maintaining the resilience and sustainability of NBCC’s operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementing a sustainable supply chain involves risks such as supplier reliability, cost escalation, disruptions, regulatory compliance, and reputational impact. Addressing these is essential for operational sustainability. NBCC is currently implementing ISO 20400: 2017 Sustainable Procurement to strengthen its supply chain practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Opportunity: Innovative and sustainable building practices offer significant potential for improving construction processes, enhancing competitiveness, encouraging collaboration, reducing costs, and expanding market opportunities. They also support regulatory compliance and risk mitigation. By embracing these practices, NBCC can deliver more sustainable solutions and strengthen its position for long-term success in the construction sector.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePoliciesExplanator:
Web-link: https://www.nbccindia.in/webEnglish/policies
Further, there could be some of the additional policies of the Company which are accessible only to employees and other internal stakeholders on account of confidentiality.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: The Company continues to advance its ESG agenda by operationalising key global standards—ISO 26000 (Social Responsibility), ISO 20400 (Sustainable Procurement), ISO 37001 (Anti-Bribery), and ISO 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety). These are being integrated across all business functions, with full implementation targeted in the current financial year. Sustainability features such as Zero Waste, Dual Piping, Rainwater Harvesting, Solar Energy, Smart Metering, and LED fixtures have been made mandatory in all future projects. Key projects aligned with these goals include: 1. Redevelopment of Sarojini Nagar, Netaji Nagar, Nauroji Nagar, New Delhi 2. Development of East Delhi Hub at Karkardooma, New Delhi 3. Redevelopment of AIIMS Western Campus, Ansari Nagar, New Delhi 4. Construction and Development of Infrastructure Facilities in the main Campus of NIT Uttarakhand 5. Redevelopment of WHO SEARO Building, New Delhi 6. Redevelopment of Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) Hostel, KG Marg, New Delhi The Company has committed to do all efforts for making all future government buildings GRIHA-compliant by 2026.

The Company continues to advance its ESG agenda by operationalising key global standards—ISO 26000 (Social Responsibility), ISO 20400 (Sustainable Procurement), ISO 37001 (Anti-Bribery), and ISO 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety). These are being integrated across all business functions, with full implementation targeted in the current financial year. Sustainability features such as Zero Waste, Dual Piping, Rainwater Harvesting, Solar Energy, Smart Metering, and LED fixtures have been made mandatory in all future projects. Key projects aligned with these goals include: 1. Redevelopment of Sarojini Nagar, Netaji Nagar, Nauroji Nagar, New Delhi 2. Development of East Delhi Hub at Karkardooma, New Delhi 3. Redevelopment of AIIMS Western Campus, Ansari Nagar, New Delhi 4. Construction and Development of Infrastructure Facilities in the main Campus of NIT Uttarakhand 5. Redevelopment of WHO SEARO Building, New Delhi 6. Redevelopment of Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) Hostel, KG Marg, New Delhi The Company has committed to do all efforts for making all future government buildings GRIHA-compliant by 2026.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT:
During the current year, the Company made steady progress on ESG targets: . ISO standards are being systematically implemented through policy restructuring, SOP alignment, and employee training. . Safety measures and ethical practices have improved, supported by audits and awareness initiatives. . Sustainable procurement processes is being embedded into project workflows. NBCC remains steadfast in advancing its ESG commitments by embedding green building features and sustainable construction practices across all major projects. Landmark developments such as GPRA, Moti Bagh (New Delhi) and the Office Complex at East Kidwai Nagar (New Delhi) stand as testimony to this approach.
Further reinforcing its sustainability credentials, the Company has secured prestigious GRIHA ratings for several new projects, including: Vanijya Bhawan, New Delhi – 5 Star Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi – 4 Star IIM Sambalpur – 4 Star AIIMS Bilaspur – 3 Star These recognitions highlight NBCC’s consistent progress towards its targets on energy efficiency, environmental responsibility, and sustainable construction. NBCC continues to recalibrate its strategies to align with evolving sustainability benchmarks and remains committed to exceeding its stated ESG goals.

During the current year, the Company made steady progress on ESG targets: . ISO standards are being systematically implemented through policy restructuring, SOP alignment, and employee training. . Safety measures and ethical practices have improved, supported by audits and awareness initiatives. . Sustainable procurement processes is being embedded into project workflows. NBCC remains steadfast in advancing its ESG commitments by embedding green building features and sustainable construction practices across all major projects. Landmark developments such as GPRA, Moti Bagh (New Delhi) and the Office Complex at East Kidwai Nagar (New Delhi) stand as testimony to this approach.
Further reinforcing its sustainability credentials, the Company has secured prestigious GRIHA ratings for several new projects, including: Vanijya Bhawan, New Delhi – 5 Star Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi – 4 Star IIM Sambalpur – 4 Star AIIMS Bilaspur – 3 Star These recognitions highlight NBCC’s consistent progress towards its targets on energy efficiency, environmental responsibility, and sustainable construction. NBCC continues to recalibrate its strategies to align with evolving sustainability benchmarks and remains committed to exceeding its stated ESG goals.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: In Annual report, Management messages highlighting ESG related disclosures.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: The Company has a Risk Management Committee to supervise the implementation of the BRSR Framework. The Risk Management Committee comprises of the following Directors as on 31.03.2025 namely: 1) Shri Anjeev Kumar Jain – Chairman, Director (Finance) 2) Shri Saleem Ahmad – Member, Director (Projects) 3) Dr. Suman Kumar- Member, Director (Commercial) Moreover, the Company has a BRSR Executive Committee comprising of senior officials namely: 1) Shri Pawan Kumar, ED (Engg.)/Chief Sustainability Officer- Chairman 2) Smt. Deepti Gambhir, Company Secretary - Member 3) Shri M.B. Singhal, ED (Finance) – Member 4) Shri C. Mani, CGM (Engg.) - Member 5) Shri Anand Kumar, GM (HRM)- Member
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: The Company has a Risk Management Committee to supervise the implementation of the BRSR Framework. The Risk Management Committee comprises of the following Directors as on 31.03.2025 namely: 1) Shri Anjeev Kumar Jain – Chairman, Director (Finance) 2) Shri Saleem Ahmed – Member, Director (Projects) 3) Dr. Suman Kumar- Member, Director (Commercial) Moreover, the Company has a BRSR Executive Committee comprising of senior officials namely: 1) Shri Pawan Kumar, ED (Engg.)/Chief Sustainability Officer- Chairman 2) Smt. Deepti Gambhir, Company Secretary - Member 3) Shri M.B. Singhal, ED (Finance) – Member 4) Shri C. Mani, CGM (Engg.) - Member 5) Shri Anand Kumar, GM (HRM)- Member

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Principles Covered – 1,4,6,8 1. Startup Board Certification Program Mastering governance in startup ecosystem 2. Director’s Certification Master Class 3. Financial Intelligence for Directors 4. Director’s Certificate Program in Corporate Governance 5. Director’s Certification in ESG Leadership 6. Orientation programme for capacity building of functional Directors (CMD/ MD + Full time Directors) of CPSEs 7. Executive development program for Senior Management
Principles Covered – 1,2,6,8 ESG for Board Members

Principles Covered – 1,2,3,5,6,8 Major topics covered during 61 training programs are as under: 1. Maximising Social Return on Investment (SROI) 2. 2 Days Conference on Transforming Supply Chain for a Sustainable Future 3. Certificate Course on Carbon Management 4. Code on Social Security 5. Director’s Certification in ESG Leadership 6. ESG for Board Members 7. Health Talk on Risk of Cardiac Disease and Lipid profile 8. Implementation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in HR Management 9. Leadership Development Program – NBCC 10. Leadership Excellence Program, Batch 1, 2, 3 & 4 11.Leadership Immersion Program 12. MDP on AI Tools for HR 13. National Conference on Industrial Relations and Awards 14. NIPM Training Program – AI Transforming Work, Workplace & Worker 15. Program on Transforming HR Function using AI/Gen AI 16. Residential Workshop on Corporate Governance 17. Retirement Readiness Program for Executives 18. Retirement Readiness Program for Non-Executives & Stress Management 19. Safety First: A Blueprint for Success in Construction 20. Session on Ethics, Governance and Preventive Vigilance for Newly Inducted Officers 21. Session on Medical Emergency 22. Session on Retirement Readiness 23. Simplified Finance for Non-Finance Professionals 24. Two Days Online Training on Quality Management System – ISO 9001:2015 25. Workshop on Meditation for Happy & Healthy Life
Principles Covered – 3,5 1. Safety First: A Blueprint for success in Construction 2. Retirement Readiness program for Non-Executives on “Retirement Readiness” and Stress Management.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheRegulatoryOrEnforceme: 1) National Stock Exchange of India Limited 2) BSE India Limited
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: The Board of Directors does not comprise the requisite number of Independent Directors & Women Independent Director during the financial year 2024-25 as required under regulation 17 of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015 and DPE guidelines on Corporate Governance. Further, since November 22, 2024; the composition of Board level committees were not in order as per SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015. In view of the above non compliances, the Stock Exchanges had levied penalty on the Company for which waiver has been requested by the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol:
Yes, NBCC has an Anti-Corruption and Anti-Bribery Policy in place. The Company is currently in the process of implementing the ISO 37001 Anti-Bribery Management System to strengthen its internal controls. The policy outlines a clear zero-tolerance approach towards any form of corruption or bribery, ensuring compliance with applicable laws and alignment with established industry practices. It clearly defines the roles and responsibilities of employees and management in preventing, identifying, and reporting unethical behaviour. To support this, regular training and awareness sessions are conducted to keep employees informed and alert. NBCC also adheres to the guidelines issued by the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC), which are designed to enhance transparency, integrity, and accountability in public sector operations. These guidelines provide a structured approach to preventive vigilance, whistleblower protection, proactive disclosures, investigations, and prosecution. In addition, NBCC has established internal policies and procedures covering Fraud Prevention and Detection, Whistleblower Mechanism, and a Code of Conduct applicable to all employees. Together, these measures serve as multiple layers of control to uphold ethical standards and ensure organizational transparency. Policy Link: Vigilance Manual: https://www.nbccindia.in/pdfData/Vig/CVC-Vigilance%20Maual-2021.pdf
Whistle Blower’s Policy: https://www.nbccindia.in/pdfData/policies/NBCC%20WHISTLE%20BLOWER%20POLICY_.pdf
Fraud Prevention & Detection Policy: https://www.nbccindia.in/pdfData/policies/NBCC_Fraud_Policy_.pdf
Anti Bribery & Anti-Corruption Policy: https://www.nbccindia.in/pdfData/policies/NBCC%20Anti%20Bribery%20and%20Anti%20Corruption%20Policy_02092024.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: NBCC is currently working towards the establishment of ISO 37001: Anti-Bribery Management System. We have already developed an Anti-corruption and Anti-Bribery policy and an Anti-Bribery Management System manual to supplement the same at finalization stage. Apart from this, we have also initiated the process of conducting routine bribery risk assessments and the same would be updated in our risk register on a periodic basis. NBCC being Central Public Sector Enterprise (CPSE), the power of appointment of Directors vests with Administrative Ministry i.e. the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MOHUA). The request, for appointment of Directors has been made to the Ministry from time to time; accordingly, four (4) Independent Directors have been appointed on the Board of NBCC after March 31, 2025. The Company is notifying the ministry about any material issues related to fines/ penalties/ action taken by regulators/law enforcement agencies/ judicial institutions, on cases of corruption and conflicts of interest.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: NBCC has a conflict-of-interest clause in place to ensure that Board Members and Senior Management act in the best interests of the organization.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Safety First: A Blueprint for success in Construction
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: NBCC has been at the forefront of encouraging and adopting sustainable construction practices. Expenditure to the tune of ₹14.94 Cr has been incurred in the following Research Projects for FY 2024–25: Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) Derivation of mix design guidelines and design parameters for Ultra-High-Performance Fibre Reinforced Concrete (UHPFRC) in building applications – IIT Roorkee Enhancing sustainability of RC frame buildings through seismic safety and design life elongation using FRP stirrups – IIT Roorkee Crystalline waterproofing admixtures for exposed surfaces: Performance under combined thermal and humidity cycles – IIT Roorkee Chloride adsorption and corrosion mitigation: Innovative solutions for improving durability and service life of RCC structures – IIT Roorkee Enhancing construction safety, sustainability, and project performance – IIT Guwahati Development of a sustainable and climate-resilient blueprint for public infrastructure – IIT Delhi National Institutes of Technology (NITs) Efficient, sustainable construction with GGBS and ROSGRA with the inclusion of waste products – NIT Delhi Development of eco-friendly heat reflective bricks and manufacturing of eco-friendly shuttering from various agro-wastes – NIT Durgapur Performance enhancement of concrete susceptible to deterioration and its retrofitting – NIT Patna Quantifying vulnerabilities, empowering solutions, and optimizing retrofitting strategies and their implementation for environmentally affected buildings – Khekra SBG, RE-VNIT Nagpur (2 projects) Other Academic & Research Institutions Study on by-products-based low-cost concrete repairing agent for extending the service life of existing structures – NSUT West Delhi Calculating compressive strength and permeability of concrete through its microstructural features – SVNIT Surat Establishment of a National Mathematics Resource Centre – Hindu College Research and development opportunities in net-zero carbon built environment – Create framework for a review and mitigation strategy through technological interventions and organizational commitments –SPA Delhi Impact assessment of water bodies, watershed management, and perennial plantation interventions on sustainable campus development - Central University of Karnataka Examining the factors contributing to accelerated building deterioration in the National Capital Region: A case study of Khekra Township
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Not Applicable, the company is not a producer of any product and is primarily engaged in Project Management Consultancy (PMC). Further, Plastic Waste, E-waste and Hazardous waste generated at the office premises is disposed through Authorised Recyclers.

Not Applicable, the company is not a producer of any product and is primarily engaged in Project Management Consultancy (PMC). Further, Plastic Waste, E-waste and Hazardous waste generated at the office premises is disposed through Authorised Recyclers.

Not Applicable, the company is not a producer of any product and is primarily engaged in Project Management Consultancy (PMC). Further, Plastic Waste, E-waste and Hazardous waste generated at the office premises is disposed through Authorised Recyclers.

Not Applicable, the company is not a producer of any product and is primarily engaged in Project Management Consultancy (PMC). Further, Plastic Waste, E-waste and Hazardous waste generated at the office premises is disposed through Authorised Recyclers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableCondu: Not applicable, the company is not a producer of any product and is primarily engaged in Project Management Consultancy (PMC).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText: EI Q-2 NBCC has a Sustainable Procurement Policy that promotes ethical conduct, social responsibility, and environmental stewardship. Suppliers are encouraged to comply with environmental laws, use eco-friendly materials, and respect human rights. The policy aims to minimize adverse impacts and maximize positive contributions through transparent and ethical procurement practices. To support this, the Company has a Standard Operating Procedure and Supplier Code of Conduct aligned with ISO 20400: Sustainable Procurement, ensuring a mechanism for transitioning to sustainable sourcing in future years. The Company is currently in the implementation phase of the SOP and will soon be able to assess and quantify sustainably sourced inputs. As a Project Management Consultancy (PMC), NBCC subcontracts project execution to contractors and does not directly procure construction materials or machinery. However, for administrative purposes in FY 2024–25, NBCC sourced 92.80% of its inputs from MSMEs, including H11.66 crore from MSMEs overall, H0.99 crore from SC/ST-owned MSMEs, and H4.61 crore from women-owned MSMEs. This reflects the Company’s commitment to inclusive sourcing in line with government procurement norms and sustainability principles.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic:
Yes, NBCC has an Equal Opportunity Policy in accordance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The policy ensures that individuals with disabilities have equitable access to employment, promotions, and training opportunities. It prohibits discrimination in recruitment, workplace conditions, and career progression on the basis of disability. The policy also outlines provisions for reasonable accommodation to enable equal participation. Furthermore, it underscores the importance of fostering an inclusive and accessible work environment by addressing and removing physical and systemic barriers.
Weblink -
https://www.nbccindia.in/pdfData/policies/Equal%20Opportunity%20Policy_12042023.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
Yes, NBCC has a Central Grievance Committee responsible for addressing employee concerns and facilitating resolutions in a fair and constructive manner. The Committee is also empowered to recommend improvements to existing rules and processes based on the nature and frequency of grievances received. Employees are encouraged to fully utilize the internal grievance redressal mechanism before seeking recourse through conciliation or legal channels. The process is designed to be accessible, with employees expected to represent themselves without legal counsel.
Not applicable in case of permanent workers and employees as the Company does not have employees and workers in this cateogry.

Yes, NBCC has a Central Grievance Committee responsible for addressing employee concerns and facilitating resolutions in a fair and constructive manner. The Committee is also empowered to recommend improvements to existing rules and processes based on the nature and frequency of grievances received. Employees are encouraged to fully utilize the internal grievance redressal mechanism before seeking recourse through conciliation or legal channels. The process is designed to be accessible, with employees expected to represent themselves without legal counsel.
Not applicable in case of permanent workers and employees as the Company does not have employees and workers in this cateogry.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes, NBCC has a Occupational Health and Safety Policy in place, along with the General Guidelines for Safety, 2016, which provide a framework for maintaining a safe and secure working environment. The Company is currently in the process of implementing ISO 45001:2018 to strengthen its occupational health and safety management system. This effort aims to improve risk management, promote preventive safety measures, and ensure compliance with relevant regulations. Regular safety audits and training programs are integral to NBCC’s approach to enhancing workplace safety.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: At NBCC project sites, hazards are consistently evaluated in accordance with the General Guidelines for Safety–2016. These evaluations are conducted by the designated safety officer or the contractor’s site supervisor to identify potential risks that may lead to accidents, injuries, or environmental harm. A Daily Inspection Report is maintained at each site to document and track these hazards. Based on the findings, the contractor’s safety team, in coordination with an NBCC-empanelled agency, develops risk mitigation strategies aimed at preventing incidents. These measures may include: a. Eliminating the hazard altogether b. Reducing the likelihood of the hazard occurring c. Reducing the severity of its consequences. Refer detailed pdf for further.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: Measures taken by NBCC to ensure a safe and healthy workplace: 1. NBCC has a Safety Policy in place, which includes the General Safety Guidelines – 2016 and a Safety Inspection Checklist. 2. The General Conditions of Contract, applicable to all construction contracts, contain a dedicated section (Section-4) titled “Labour Safety, Health & Sanitary Rules and Regulations including forms,” outlining labour safety provisions to be implemented by the contractor. For further points please refer our BRSR Report available on our website.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: 1. Periodic safety audits are conducted by NBCC’s Safety Division to assess and enhance safety compliance at project sites. For further points please refer our BRSR Report availabe on our website.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: All the Regional Business Group / Strategic Business Groups of Company ensures that all the statutory dues are deducted and deposited on time by the value chain partners.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Identifying and engaging stakeholders is an essential part of NBCC’s operations. The Company follows a structured approach that includes identifying both internal and external stakeholders, assessing their concerns and interests, evaluating their level of influence, exploring potential collaborations, and periodically reviewing engagement strategies. This systematic process enables NBCC to understand and address stakeholder expectations effectively, thereby strengthening relationships. By prioritizing engagement with customers, employees, communities, governments, suppliers, shareholders, and other relevant groups, NBCC supports improved project outcomes and enhanced stakeholder value.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: NBCC follows a structured approach to engage employees in consultations related to internal policies. At present, this consultation process is limited to internal stakeholders. Feedback gathered through these engagements is systematically reviewed and incorporated into decision-making processes. In cases where consultations on economic, environmental, or social matters are delegated, the feedback is formally communicated to the Board, ensuring that the Board remains informed and involved in relevant decisions. Further, for other stakeholders, we have proces of formal meetings and dialogues i.e. Manthan, Samvaad and also informal discussions are held.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: stakeholder consultation was an integral part of the previous reporting period and was actively utilized to support the identification and management of key environmental and social topics. NBCC follows a structured stakeholder engagement approach that includes identifying internal and external stakeholders, assessing their concerns, and evaluating their influence. Feedback gathered during these consultations played a pivotal role in the materiality assessment process, helping to determine priority issues. These insights were incorporated into the development and refinement of NBCC’s policies and initiatives to ensure alignment with stakeholder expectations. Where consultations pertained to economic, environmental, or social matters, the feedback was formally communicated to the Board, reinforcing informed and responsive decision-making at the highest level.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: This parameter is not applicable to NBCC, as there have been no such instances reported during the period.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: 1) Annual Shareholder Meeting 2) Investor and Analyst Meet 3) Press releases and Newspaper Publications 4) Annual Reports 5) Communication with Stock Exchanges 6) Email & Written Communication
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Focus is on creating shareholders wealth. Key topics: 1) Financial Performance 2) Business Strategy 3) Business Performance 4) Corporate Governance 5) Market Opportunity 6) Providing Future Guidance
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: 1) Employee newsletters 2) Intranet Portal 3) Cultural events, functions & interactions, and review meetings 4) Trainings and performance management system 5) Functional and cross functional committees 6) Emails and other forms of written communication
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherFrequencyOfEngag: Periodically / As and when required / Event based
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1) Information 2) Trainings 3) Skill Development 4) Awareness and activities 5) Business activities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: 1) Direct consumer calls 2) Client/Customer satisfaction surveys 3) Complaint handling & feedback 4) Marketing and Advertising 5) Electronic Communication
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1) Customers satisfaction survey 2) Grievance Redressal 3) Business Activities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: 1) Annual Reports 2) Communications with Regulatory Bodies 3) Formal Dialogues 4) Physical Meetings
1) Contractor, Consultant, and Supplier Meetings 2) Regular interactions via phone, email, and in-person 3) Vendor Grievance Portal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1) Community Development, 2) Skill Development 3) Corporate Social Responsibility
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: NBCC (India) Limited has established a comprehensive grievance redressal framework under its Human Rights Policy and Internal and External Grievance Redressal Policies, designed to address concerns raised by both internal and external stakeholders. These mechanisms aim to uphold dignity, fairness, and inclusivity, and ensure that complaints related to human rights are handled transparently, confidentially, and with due process. The organization promotes awareness through structured orientation programs that include dedicated modules on Human Rights, reinforcing its commitment to fostering a respectful and inclusive work environment. Specific roles, such as Site Project Engineers (Civil), also undergo training to ensure wider understanding of these policies. In addition, the organization’s POSH Policy, compliant with relevant laws, contributes to the broader human rights framework by safeguarding against sexual harassment and ensuring a safe workplace for all employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: NBCC (India) Limited’s POSH Policy ensures a safe and respectful workplace for women by preventing and addressing sexual harassment. The policy applies to all employees, including temporary and contract workers, and covers incidents occurring both within and outside office premises. An Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) is constituted as per legal requirements to handle complaints with confidentiality and impartiality, conduct thorough investigations, and recommend appropriate disciplinary actions. The policy strictly prohibits retaliation against complainants or witnesses and emphasizes the importance of confidentiality and fairness, including addressing false accusations. To prevent adverse consequences for complainants, several proactive measures are in place. These include interim reliefs such as transferring the aggrieved woman or the respondent to another workplace within the organization to ensure a safe environment. The aggrieved woman may also be granted a leave of absence of up to three months, in addition to her regular leave entitlements. The ICC may recommend other specific reliefs as per applicable regulations. Regular awareness and training programs are conducted to educate employees and foster a culture of respect, safety, and accountability. The organization is committed to supporting aggrieved individuals through comprehensive services and safeguards throughout the complaint process.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: All necessary requirement is being incorporated in the business processes and the same are getting modified to best suits and addresses the human rights.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: Currently, no Human Rights Due Diligence has been conducted during the reporting period. However, the Company is in the process of implementing ISO 26000, which will enable operationalization of Human Rights Due Diligence going forward.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: NBCC (India) Ltd integrates energy-efficient practices such as solar energy, LED lighting, and smart metering into its projects to reduce energy use and associated emissions. These measures are implemented during the planning and execution stages to support long-term emission reduction. The company continues to explore sustainable building solutions to further minimise its carbon footprint.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: The organization regularly practices recycling and proper disposal techniques. E-waste and paper waste are collected, sorted and supplied to respective approved vendors. Furthermore, NBCC complies with relevant waste management regulations and guidelines, including proper handling and disposal of hazardous waste.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:LocationOfOperationsOrOffices: Indo-Bangladesh Border Fencing Works, Dampa Tiger Reserve Forest, Mizoram. (BP No. 2313/2RI to 2305)
Indo-Bangladesh Border Fencing Works, East Jaintia Hills, Meghalaya (BP No. - 1301/4S to 1316/M)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: NBCC (India) Ltd does not have a dedicated Business Continuity Plan (BCP). However, the company maintains a robust risk management framework aimed at ensuring operational continuity and resilience. Processes include proactive identification, assessment, and mitigation of risks at both enterprise and project levels, overseen by a Risk Management Committee. Regular risk monitoring, review, and integration with strategic planning help safeguard critical operations against disruptions. This framework supports NBCC’s commitment to maintaining business stability and preparedness across all functions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText:
As per IMF data for the year 2025, the Purchasing Power Parity (PPP) value for India has been taken as 20.66. Since this value has remained consistent for two consecutive years, the PPP value for the previous year has also been restated accordingly, in line with SEBI’s guideline
EI Q-7 The calculation methodology for Scope 2 emissions was revised in accordance with the GHG Protocol. Consequently, the emission values for the previous year have been restated to ensure consistency. Emission factors from the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) report were applied for both consecutive years.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: NBCC (India) Limited uses a dual grievance redressal system comprising its internal Grievance Redressal SOP and the government’s CPGRAMS platform. The internal SOP outlines a transparent and structured process for addressing grievances from both internal stakeholders (employees, senior management) and external stakeholders (contractors, vendors, customers). It defines reporting channels, escalation levels, and timelines, ensuring accountability and proper documentation. Internal grievances can be reported verbally, in writing, or via email, escalating up to the Chief Grievance Redressal Officer (CGRO) if unresolved. External grievances follow a similar process through written communication or email. In parallel, NBCC also addresses public grievances through CPGRAMS (Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System), a government portal used to receive, track, and act on complaints from the general public and communities. Relevant departments review each case and take appropriate action, supporting transparency and responsiveness.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText:
EI Q-5 Note: The Company has considered the number of new employees hired during the year and applied a methodology wherein this figure is divided by the total remuneration provided, to calculate the above percentage.
In CSR, As the location being an aspirational district maximum population is from the vulnerable and marginalized groups

Sponsoring establishment of Eco Health Park at Tengunia Dol Utshab Ground, Tengunia Arunodaya Pallimangal Samity, Purba Medinipur, West Bengal
Sponsoring development of Yoga, Multi Gym and Meditation Center, Uttar Dulalpur Yuba Sangha, Purba Medinipur, West Bengal

Sponsoring development of Radio Station, Baramullah, J&K
Sponsoring procurement of one ambulance and hearse car, Manab Jati Kalyan Foundation, Kolkata, WB
Sponsoring procurement of one ambulance and setting up of a health centre, Silsury, Mamit, Mizoram
Sponsoring construction of accommodation for Cow Sewaks at Kamdhenu Arogya Sansthan, Nuh, (An Aspirational District), Haryana.
Sponsoring nutritious meal to 300 underprivileged Tribal Girls studying at Kalinga Institute of Social Sciences, Bhubaneshwar
Sponsoring construction community Hall for multipurpose use including Pre-School at CBI Colony, Vasant Vihar, New Delhi.
Sponsoring procurement of blankets distribution to the poor Janpadh, Shahjahanpur, UP.
Sponsoring procurement of health equipments for Arohi Society, Nainital, Uttarakhand
Sponsoring Facility-based Pulmonary Rehabilitation Intervention Package (PRIP) for individuals with post-tuberculosis lung disease (PTLD), New Delhi

Sponsoring of Aids & Assistive Devices to Divyangjan and Senior Citizens, ALIMCO
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:FromWhichMarginalizedOrVulnera: 1) Women entrepreneur 2) MSME SC /ST owned vendor
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: NBCC’s Grievance Redressal SOP provides a transparent, efficient, and equitable framework for addressing grievances from internal (employees, senior management) and external stakeholders (contractors, vendors, customers, etc.). It outlines a structured process with multiple reporting channels—verbal, written, or email—and defines clear steps for resolution at various levels. The procedure is supported by a Grievance Redressal Matrix involving immediate superiors, nodal representatives, the Chief Grievance Redressal Officer (CGRO), and the Grievance Redressal Committee (GRC), where applicable. Defined timelines and role clarity ensure accountability and timely resolution. The SOP also includes an escalation mechanism for unresolved cases, a feedback loop for continuous improvement, and emphasizes confidentiality, with comprehensive documentation and tracking to ensure transparency and compliance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: No such incidents warrantying corrective actions, occured during the financial year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: This parameter is not applicable to NBCC, as the company does not manufacture products or provide services that require consumer education on safe and responsible usage
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: This parameter is not applicable to NBCC, as the company does not provide essential consumer services where risk of disruption or discontinuation notifications are required.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableProdu: This parameter is not applicable to NBCC, as the company does not manufacture or sell products and does not conduct consumer satisfaction surveys in relation to its services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableCarry: This parameter is not applicable to NBCC, as the company does not manufacture or sell products and does not conduct consumer satisfaction surveys in relation to its services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple9ExplanatoryText:
NOTES TO BUSINESS RESPONSIBILITY AND SUSTAINABILITY REPORT FOR FY 2024-25:
. The Company has provided leadership indicators only to the extent of information readily available in this regard. However, the Company is also taking steps so that the leadership indicators can be provided in subsequent years. . Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) is an online platform available to the citizens 24x7 to lodge their grievances to the public authorities on any subject related to service delivery. It is a single portal connected to all the Ministries/Departments of Government of India and States. Accordingly, any stakeholder group, identified or unidentified by the Company, can access the aforesaid online platform for their Grievance Redressal, if any. . Financial implications of the risk or opportunity on Material Business Conduct issues as identified in qualitative/quantitative terms are monitored by the Board/respective Committee periodically. . The Company indulges in constant dialogue periodically with various value chain partners to ensure adherence to NGRBC principles in letter and spirit. . Information provided in the Principle 6 do not include data in respect of sub-contracting services availed by NBCC from various contractors as they are value chain partners having separate independent identity with their distinct business and sustainability characteristics vis-à-vis the Company. . Only non-executive (Group C&D) employees of the Company are covered in the information w.r.t. workers in this report. . ‘Revenue from operations’ has been considered for ‘Turnover’ mentioned in this Report.
